A1.q: There ________ a book on the table.
There ________ three cats in the garden.

I ________ TV when the phone rang.
If it rains, we ________ at home.
I have lived here ________ five years.
He is as ________ as his father.

B1.q: He told me that he ________ busy.
If I had money, I ________ an island.
By the time we get there, the film ________.
You don't need to come ________ you want to.
That is the man ________ car was stolen.
I wish I ________ a bigger house.
Do you happen to know where ________?

I'll call you as soon as I ________ home.

B2.q: By the time we arrived, they ________.
o: Both A and B are correct

B2.q: Zaznacz poprawne kontynuacje: If you had asked me...
o: I could have given you a hand.

B2.q: It’s high time you ________ a job.
o: None of the above is correct

B2.q: You ________ have called me! I was worried.
o: A and B are logically correct here

B2.q: Które zdania są poprawne? (Zaznacz wszystkie)
o: Hardly I had left the house when it started to rain.
Hardly had I left the house when it started to rain.
Scarcely had I left the house than it started to rain.
No sooner had I left the house than it started to rain.

B2.q: He is believed ________ the country.
o: Both A and B are correct in different contexts

B2.q: She had her car ________ yesterday.
Które z opcji pasują w lukę: I’d rather you ________ here.
Despite ________ hard, he failed.
o: Both A and C are correct

B2.q: Zaznacz poprawne formy czasownika po zwrocie: There is no point in...
It's no use ________ over spilled milk.
We’ll go out ________ it rains.
She enjoys ________ a lot of responsibilities.
o: A and B are both acceptable

B2.q: Wybierz poprawne inwersje: Only after the meeting...
The project needs ________ by Friday.
I object ________ to like that.
o: Both B and C are correct

B2.q: Not only ________ late, but he also forgot the documents.
He succeeded ________ passing the exam.
o: Both A and C are acceptable

B2.q: Wybierz poprawne: I am not used...
I didn't ________ to like vegetables as a kid.

C1.q: By the time the police ____, the thieves ____ their getaway, leaving absolutely ____ behind.
o: Both B and C are grammatically and logically correct

C1.q: Zaznacz wszystkie poprawne formy zdania warunkowego II i III typu:
o: If I knew you were coming, I would bake a cake.
Had I known you were coming, I would have baked a cake.
If I had known you were coming, I would have baked a cake.
If I know you are coming, I will bake a cake.

C1.q: ____ had he finished his speech ____ the audience ____ in rapturous applause.
o: None of the above works perfectly

C1.q: I would rather you ____ me the truth yesterday. Now, I ____ entirely ____.
Wybierz poprawne zwroty o podobnym znaczeniu: We are completely unaccustomed to...
He ____ to have embezzled the funds, but there is ____ proof, so he remains ____.
o: None of the choices fits correctly seamlessly

C1.q: Only after the meeting ____ that she ____ the most crucial details of the ____.
o: did I realize, had omitted, report
had I realized, has omitted, report
Both A and C are correct forms of inversion

C1.q: Zaznacz poprawne opcje zaczynające się od Were/Had:
o: Were it not for his intervention, the company would go bankrupt.
Had it not been for his intervention, the company would have gone bankrupt.
Were he to intervene, the company wouldn't bankrupt.
Had he intervened, the company would go bankrupt.

C1.q: The committee is on the ____ of ____ a decision that will impact everyone ____.
o: None of the above is idiomatic

C1.q: Much ____ I respect his achievements, I ____ agree with the ____ he took.
o: Options A and B are perfectly synonymous here

C1.q: Wybierz poprawne użycie the subjunctive:
o: It is imperative that the CEO be present.
I demanded that he arrives on time.
It is crucial that she arrive early.
The doctor suggested to take the medicine.

C1.q: She was completely taken ____ by the news, rendering her ____ for a few ____.
There is a conspicuous ____ of evidence, so we must ____ this case ____.
o: Both A and B collate somewhat correctly, but A is standard legal jargon

C1.q: Wybierz prawidłowe synonimy do słowa 'lacking':
Try not to beat around the ____; just ____ me the truth ____.
The noise from the street is driving me up the ____; I simply ____ concentrate on my ____.
Zaznacz idiomatyczne określenie sedna przyczyny:
The manager decided to lay ____ fifty workers, causing an ____ among the ____.
I haven't got a ____ what you're talking about, so please ____ it ____.
o: A and B are both perfectly natural

C1.q: The government aims to crack ____ on corruption, introducing ____ penalties for ____.
o: Both A and C are entirely correct

C2.q: The proposal was passed, albeit with some ____, as the board had ____ over its financial ____.
o: Options A and B represent excellent C2 collocations

C2.q: Wybierz prawidłowe słówka opisujące zachwyt/fascynację (synonimy do stunning):
The argument he put forward was legally ____, leaving the opposing counsel grasping ____ ____.
It's a ____ conclusion that he will win, though his opponents ____ to admit ____.
o: A and C are both standard idioms

C2.q: Zaznacz poprawne czasowniki obrazujące zniekształcenie prawdy:
This new evidence throws the case wide ____, unraveling the ____ narrative completely and ____.
o: None of the above fits the idiom perfectly

C2.q: His remarks were tantamount ____ an insult, causing a deep ____ within the ____.
o: Both A and B are acceptable

C2.q: The economy is teetering on the ____ of collapse, prompting ____ measures from the ____.
o: All options are perfectly synonymous and correct

C2.q: Wybierz czasowniki łączące się poprawnie z preposition 'on/upon':
The old rules have fallen into ____, superseded by more ____ guidelines that reflect modern ____.
o: A and C are both extremely advanced synonyms for disuse

C2.q: It's difficult to gauge the ____ of the disaster, given the ____ devastation across the ____.
We must ____ out these systemic issues at the source before they ____ our entire ____.
o: Both A and B use horticultural metaphors correctly here

C2.q: Zaznacz przymiotniki trafnie opisujące 'krytyczny/zgryźliwy dowcip lub język':
The debate finally ____ down to one core issue, stripping away the ____ rhetoric and ____.
o: A incorporates the correct phrasal verb

C2.q: I was completely ____ by the complexity of the task, requiring a ____ approach to even ____ it.
o: Options A and B are entirely interchangeable here

C2.q: She took a ____ view of my attempts to help, feeling ____ rather than ____.
o: Both A and B represent correct collocations for negative perception

C2.q: Zaznacz poprawne wyrażenia oznaczające 'wstydliwie się wykręcać / oszukiwać w sprawie czasu':
The whole story was a figment of his ____, devised to ____ the authorities and ____ time.
o: All are grammatically sound, but A is the only correct idiomatic phrasing

C2.q: They were leaving the company in ____, citing a toxic culture that ____ all sense of ____.
o: None of the above options have a correct herd-animal idiom

C2.q: He has a ____ for making people feel comfortable, effortlessly ____ tension in any ____.
o: Both B and C represent pristine idiomatic English
